toolArgs.description: Fix and rerun stackalloc nint test

data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>`.** Both work as expected:

- `Span<nint> span = stackalloc nint[4];` ✅
- `Span<nuint> span = stackalloc nuint[3];` ✅

On this 64-bit system, `nint` is 8 bytes, so the spans hold pointer-sized signed/unsigned integers with full `Span` semantics (indexing, bounds checking, etc.). This has been supported since C# 9 / .NET 5 when `nint`/`nuint` were introduced as first-class types.
